LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS OVERRULED IN PART, AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF DUTY OF LOYALTY AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF DUTY OF CONFIDENTIALITY. FAILURE TO STATE A BASIS FOR ASSERTIONS THAT THE CROSS-DEFENDANT HAD A FIDUCIARY RESPONSIBILITY TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT. NO FACTS TO SUPPORT A CONFIDENTIAL RELATIONSHIP. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CAUSES OF ACTION, NOT TIME BARRED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO PLEAD BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY AS TO TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT AND FACTS CONSTITUTING BREACH. FACTS MUST BE ALLEGED REGARDING EXISTENCE OF CONFIDENTIAL RELATIONSHIP, THAT A COMMUNICATION WAS MADE IN CONFIDENCE AND DISCLOSED, AND NATURE OF DUTY OF UNDIVIDED LOYALTY. THE COURT ADDS THAT COUNSEL'S STIPULATION ON THE RECORD TO AN AGREEMENT OF ONE MONTH CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE IS ALSO PART OF THE COURT'S ORDER. MS. CALABRIA TO SERVE MR. MARIAM WITH AMENDED COMPLAINT BY FAX OR EMAIL. (THE COURT REASONS RELATION BACK DOCTRINE APPLIES. 2007 ORIGINAL CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS SUFFICIENT.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT-HARRISON, CSR #4609

REPLY SEPARATE STATMEENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF X-COMPLAINTS' OPPOS TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMNT/ADJUDCIATION FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT BONHACH, JEFFREY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BONHACH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVAE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED REGARDING THE NATURE OF CONTRACT, AND WHETHER BONHACH WAS AN AT WILL EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE LANGUAGE OF THE 6/18/04 EMAIL IS AMBIGUOUS, REFERENCING AN "AT WILL" RELATIONSHIP AND THEN REFERS TO PLAINTIFF AS A CONSULTANT. BOTH PARTIES' DEPOSITIONS SUPPORT BONHACH DISCRETION TO CONDUCT HIMSELF SIMILAR TO AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR (BONHACH DEPOS PP. 103, 118; LEFF DEPO PG. 57 LINE, 10 THRU PG. 58, LINE 19, ALSO HIS DECLARATION AT PARAGRAPH 8.) TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER CONTRACT BREACHED AND WHETHER DAMAGES SUSTAINED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHEN EXACTLY THE BREACH OCCURRED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO ALL SEPARATE "ISSUES." PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW HOW THE EVIDENCE COMPLETELY DISPOSES OF A SIMPLE CAUSE OF ACTION OR ISSUE OF DUTY. (CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1); CRC 3.1112, 3.1113. THE COURT ADDS: EVID OBJEC. TO DECL. OF JOHANNA CALABRIA SUSTAINED AS TO OBJEC. #2; EVID OBJEC. TO DECL. LEFF, SUSTAINED AS TO ## 12, 13, AND 14, OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINDER; OPPOSITION TO REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OVERRULED, COURT TAKES NOTICE OF STATEMENT AND FACTS IN DOC. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BONHACH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVAE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. Appearances: Craig J. Mariam, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP (619) 696-6700, appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Jeffrey Bonhach and moving party; Johanna Calabria, Esq. Calabria Law Group (415) 781-7400, appearing for Cross-Complainants Malibu Pier Partners, Alexander Leff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of fact raised regarding the nature of contract, and whether Bonhach was an at will employee or independent contractor. The language of the 6/18/04 email is ambiguous, referencing an "at will" relationship and then refers to plaintiff as a consultant. Both parties' depositions support Bonhach discretion to conduct himself similar to an independent contractor (Bonhach depos pp. 103, 118; Leff depo pg. 57 line, 10 thru pg. 58, line 19, Also his declaration at paragraph 8.) Triable issues of fact whether contract breached and whether damages sustained. Triable issue of fact as to when exactly the breach occurred. Summary adjudication denied as to all separate "issues." Plaintiff fails to show how the evidence completely disposes of a simple cause of action or issue of duty. (CCP section 437c(f)(1); CRC 3.1112, 3.1113. The Court adds: Evid Objec. to Decl. Of Johanna Calabria sustained as to Objec. #2; Evid Objec. to Decl. Leff, sustained as to ## 12, 13, and 14, overruled as to the remainder; Opposition to Request for Judicial Notice overruled, Court takes notice of statement and facts in doc. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-09-2009 Continued To Sep-21-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Mandatory Transfer Of Venue
Off Calendar May-29-2009 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Craig J. Mariam, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, (619( 696-6700, x 7492, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant and moving party; Johanna Calabria, of the Calabria Law Group (415) 781-7400, appearing for Cross-complainants MPP and Alexander Leff, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Strike is denied. No showing that the cross-complaint arises from protected activity. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Craig J. Mariam, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, (619( 696-6700, x 7492, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant and moving party; Johanna Calabria, of the Calabria Law Group (415) 781-7400, appearing for Cross-complainants MPP and Alexander Leff, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action for Breach of Contract. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for Breach of Duty of Loyalty and third cause of action for Breach of Duty of Confidentiality. Failure to state a basis for assertions that the cross-defendant had a fiduciary responsibility to cross-complainant. No facts to support a confidential relationship. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. (Defense counsel will prepare Order.) (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Johanna Calabria, Esq. of the Calabria Law Group (415) 781-7400, appearing for Defendants/Cross-Complainants Malibu Pier Partners, and Alexander Leff, and moving party; Craig J. Mariam, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, (619) 696-6700 x 7492, Appearing thru CourtCall, appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Jeffrey Bonhach, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Causes of action, not time barred. Cross-complainant to plead breach of contract with greater specificity as to terms of the agreement and facts constituting breach. Facts must be alleged regarding existence of confidential relationship, that a communication was made in confidence and disclosed, and nature of duty of undivided loyalty. The Court adds that Counsel's stipulation on the record to an agreement of one month continuance of the trial date is also part of the Court's Order. Ms. Calabria to serve Mr. Mariam with Amended Complaint by Fax or email. (The Court reasons relation back doctrine applies. 2007 original cross-complaint was sufficient.) (302CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison, CSR 4609
